items.q: What is the scope of a local variable?
items.correct: Accessible only within the function/block where it is declared.
items.random_wrong: Stored on a local computer rather than a server.
items.why: Students confuse 'local' with 'on this computer'. In programming, scope refers to the CODE REGION where the variable name resolves - local variables vanish when the function returns.

items.q: What is the key difference between a compiler and an interpreter?
items.correct: A compiler translates the whole program first; an interpreter executes line by line.
items.misconception: A compiler is a person, an interpreter is software.
items.why: Students confuse the human meanings with technical ones. In CS, both are programs. Compilers produce an executable; interpreters run source directly each time.

items.q: What happens when 11111111 + 1 is computed in an 8-bit register?
items.correct: Overflow - the result requires 9 bits but only 8 are available.
items.why: Students miss that registers have fixed width. The mathematical result is 9 bits, but the 8-bit register drops the carry-out, producing 00000000 with an overflow flag.

items.q: Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm was published in:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1959. “1945” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's LIFO. “FIFO” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Year Dijkstra published his shortest path algorithm?
items.why: The real figure is 1959 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
